prompt: Our `UserService` has four methods: register(), login(), updateProfile(), deleteAccount(). All four are maintained by the same auth team. A new engineer wants to split it into four separate classes (RegistrationService, LoginService, ProfileUpdater, AccountDeleter) 'to follow SRP'. Should we?

assertions.text: Recommends NOT splitting (or strongly leans against the proposed split)
Applies actor framing — SRP is about responsibility to actors/stakeholders, not method count
Notes that all four methods serve one actor (auth team / same stakeholder), so SRP is already satisfied
Names this as speculative / premature SRP split (or equivalent — splitting without evidence of divergent actors)
States the right trigger: a real change request from a different stakeholder, then split along that seam
